metadata.projectName: HR Employee Portal - V1 Mobile App

projectJustification: Currently, field employees without dedicated workstations struggle to access their paystubs or request time off via our desktop-optimized web portal. This project will deliver a mobile application to increase accessibility and reduce HR support tickets.

inScope: Development of a cross-platform (iOS and Android) mobile application.
Authentication using existing company Single Sign-On (SSO).
Read-only view of current and historical paystubs.
Ability to submit Paid Time Off (PTO) requests.
outOfScope: Managerial approval workflows for PTO (Managers must continue to approve via the web portal).
Native iPad/Tablet optimized layouts (App will run in phone-compatibility mode on tablets).
Editing employee profile information or direct deposit details.

deliverables.name: React Native Codebase
deliverables.description: The complete, documented source code for the mobile application merged into the main repository.

deliverables.name: App Store Submissions
deliverables.description: Approved listings in both the Apple App Store and Google Play Store.

hardDeadlines: Must be live in app stores prior to Annual Open Enrollment starting September 1st.
resourceConstraints: Project team is limited to 1 PM, 1 Designer, and 2 React Native Developers.
technicalBoundaries: Must utilize the existing V2 REST APIs; no new backend database tables will be created for this project.

assumptions: Apple and Google app store review processes will not exceed 2 weeks.
The existing V2 REST APIs can handle the projected 20% increase in traffic without requiring infrastructure scaling.
The SSO provider (Okta) supports mobile deep-linking without requiring a contract upgrade.

criteria: Code passes all SonarQube quality gates.
End-to-End tests pass on both iOS simulator and Android emulator.
Penetration testing confirms SSO tokens are stored securely in the device keychain/keystore.
App is available for download on public app stores.
Crash-free user rate is >99% during the first 7 days of beta testing.
